response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable individual contributor with a broad technical background, but this resume presents no evidence of executive or engineering leadership experience. The CTO role requires demonstrated team scaling (10–30+ people), board-level communication, org design, and production multi-agent AI systems operating in regulated environments. The candidate's most senior title is Staff Software Engineer; there is no indication of ever managing, hiring, or growing a team. Their only AI-agent exposure is a vague, lightly described current engagement of roughly one year. The mismatch with the role's requirements is fundamental, not a gap that interview performance could bridge.
strengths: Distributed-systems and high-throughput microservices experience at Dunnhumby, operating at millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async DLang services.
Broad, cross-domain technical fluency spanning backend, frontend, mobile, and embedded systems over nearly two decades of hands-on engineering.
Current involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq signals early engagement with agentic workflow patterns.
concerns: Zero management or engineering leadership experience — no evidence of leading, hiring, mentoring, or scaling a team at any point in their career.
Agentic AI experience is limited to one vague, recent mention with no detail on production scale, architecture ownership, evaluation infrastructure, or regulated-environment operation.

No executive-layer markers present: no board communication, no budget or headcount ownership, no org design, no delivery-partner governance, no mention of technical roadmap communication to stakeholders.
